terms.definition: Deficiency, excess or imbalance of energy, protein and nutrients causing measurable adverse effects on body and function.
terms.exampleSentence: Screening showed she was at high risk of malnutrition following her recent hospital admission.
terms.contextNotes: Use MUST tool to grade risk; document weight and BMI.

terms.definition: Insufficient intake of energy or nutrients leading to weight loss, wasting or micronutrient deficiency.
terms.exampleSentence: Chronic undernutrition had contributed to his unintentional weight loss over six months.
terms.contextNotes: A subtype of malnutrition; not synonymous with malnutrition overall.

terms.definition: Abnormal or excessive fat accumulation that presents a risk to health, commonly defined as BMI ≥ 30 kg/m².
terms.exampleSentence: Class II obesity was confirmed and a structured weight management plan was discussed.
terms.contextNotes: Use neutral, person-first language with patients.

terms.definition: Autoimmune destruction of pancreatic beta cells causing absolute insulin deficiency and lifelong insulin dependence.
terms.exampleSentence: She has lived with type 1 diabetes since childhood and uses an insulin pump.
terms.contextNotes: Carbohydrate counting central to dietetic input.

terms.definition: Chronic condition of insulin resistance and relative insulin deficiency, often linked to lifestyle and central adiposity.
terms.exampleSentence: His type 2 diabetes was newly diagnosed with an HbA1c of 62 mmol/mol.
terms.contextNotes: Remission possible with weight loss; reference NICE guidance.

terms.definition: Autoimmune enteropathy triggered by dietary gluten causing villous atrophy and malabsorption in genetically susceptible individuals.
terms.exampleSentence: A strict, lifelong gluten-free diet was advised following her coeliac disease diagnosis.
terms.contextNotes: Confirm via serology and biopsy before dietary advice.

terms.definition: Functional gastrointestinal disorder with abdominal pain and altered bowel habit in the absence of organic disease.
terms.exampleSentence: A trial of the low FODMAP diet was offered for her irritable bowel syndrome.
terms.contextNotes: Exclude red flags before dietary restriction.

terms.definition: Chronic transmural inflammatory bowel disease that may affect any part of the gastrointestinal tract from mouth to anus.
terms.exampleSentence: Exclusive enteral nutrition was used to induce remission in his Crohn's disease.
terms.contextNotes: Watch for strictures and B12 deficiency post-resection.

terms.definition: Chronic inflammatory bowel disease confined to the colonic mucosa, typically presenting with bloody diarrhoea.
terms.exampleSentence: Iron deficiency anaemia was managed alongside her ulcerative colitis flare.
terms.contextNotes: Monitor iron, vitamin D and bone health.

terms.definition: Difficulty swallowing food, fluids or saliva that may compromise nutrition, hydration or airway safety.
terms.exampleSentence: Following speech and language assessment, she was placed on a level 5 minced and moist diet for dysphagia.
terms.contextNotes: Coordinate with SLT; document IDDSI level on plan.

terms.definition: Potentially fatal shifts in fluids and electrolytes that may occur in malnourished patients receiving artificial or oral refeeding.
terms.exampleSentence: She was identified as high risk of refeeding syndrome and feeds were started cautiously.
terms.contextNotes: Use NICE refeeding criteria; replace thiamine first.

terms.definition: Eating disorder characterised by restriction of intake, low body weight and intense fear of weight gain.
terms.exampleSentence: Weekly meal planning was agreed as part of her treatment for anorexia nervosa.
terms.contextNotes: Avoid weight talk early; follow MEED guidance.

terms.definition: Eating disorder marked by recurrent binge eating followed by compensatory behaviours such as vomiting or laxative misuse.
terms.exampleSentence: Regular eating patterns and food diaries were introduced for her bulimia nervosa.
terms.contextNotes: Check potassium and dental health; refer for CBT-ED.

terms.definition: Avoidant/restrictive food intake disorder involving persistent failure to meet nutritional needs without weight or shape concerns.
terms.exampleSentence: ARFID was suspected given her sensory-based food avoidance and limited intake range.
terms.contextNotes: Distinguish from anorexia nervosa; no body image driver.

terms.definition: Reproducible immune-mediated adverse reaction to a specific food protein, which may be IgE or non-IgE mediated.
terms.exampleSentence: An adrenaline auto-injector was prescribed because of her peanut food allergy and risk of anaphylaxis.
terms.contextNotes: Differentiate from intolerance; document allergens clearly.

terms.definition: Inability to digest lactose due to reduced lactase activity, causing bloating, wind and diarrhoea after dairy intake.
terms.exampleSentence: Symptoms resolved when low-lactose dairy alternatives were introduced for her lactose intolerance.
terms.contextNotes: Ensure adequate calcium and vitamin D intake.

terms.definition: Inherited metabolic disorder of phenylalanine metabolism requiring lifelong low-phenylalanine diet to prevent neurological damage.
terms.exampleSentence: Her phenylketonuria has been managed with a strict low-protein diet and prescribed amino acid formula.
terms.contextNotes: Coordinate with metabolic team; lifelong follow-up.

terms.definition: Progressive loss of kidney function over months or years, classified by eGFR and albuminuria categories.
terms.exampleSentence: Dietary potassium and phosphate were reviewed in line with his stage 4 chronic kidney disease.
terms.contextNotes: Tailor protein, potassium and phosphate to CKD stage.

terms.definition: Clinical syndrome of heavy proteinuria, hypoalbuminaemia, oedema and hyperlipidaemia caused by glomerular disease.
terms.exampleSentence: A reduced-salt diet was advised to help manage oedema in his nephrotic syndrome.
terms.contextNotes: Limit added salt; avoid high-protein loading.

terms.definition: Elevated total or LDL cholesterol in the blood, increasing the risk of atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease.
terms.exampleSentence: A Mediterranean-style diet was discussed to help manage her hypercholesterolaemia.
terms.contextNotes: Consider familial hypercholesterolaemia in young patients.

terms.definition: Raised fasting triglyceride concentration in blood, associated with cardiovascular risk and, when severe, pancreatitis.
terms.exampleSentence: Alcohol reduction was advised due to severe hypertriglyceridaemia and pancreatitis risk.
terms.contextNotes: Limit alcohol and refined carbohydrate; review fasting lipids.

terms.definition: Carbohydrate intolerance first recognised during pregnancy, diagnosed by oral glucose tolerance test thresholds.
terms.exampleSentence: Following her abnormal OGTT, gestational diabetes was diagnosed and dietary advice provided.
terms.contextNotes: Plan postnatal review; future T2DM risk increased.

terms.definition: Severe, life-threatening systemic hypersensitivity reaction with airway, breathing or circulation involvement.
terms.exampleSentence: She had previously experienced anaphylaxis after accidental exposure to peanut.
terms.contextNotes: Confirm allergy plan and auto-injector training.

terms.definition: Progressive, generalised loss of skeletal muscle mass and strength associated with ageing or disease.
terms.exampleSentence: Resistance exercise and protein-rich meals were advised to address her sarcopenia.
terms.contextNotes: Aim 1.0–1.2 g/kg/day protein where appropriate.

terms.definition: Complex metabolic syndrome of ongoing muscle loss, often with fat loss, driven by underlying illness such as cancer.
terms.exampleSentence: Cancer cachexia was contributing to his profound weight loss despite oral intake.
terms.contextNotes: Energy and protein dense foods; manage symptoms holistically.
